methods: Fold-contained LOO (train-only panel standardization + train-fit infiltration residualization); held-out cross-cohort validation trains axis+logistic map on one cohort and predicts the other. All leakage-free.

riaz.note: At n=5v5 AUROC=0.40 is statistically indistinguishable from chance (0.5); medians overlap. This is NON-REPLICATION / no separation, NOT a sign reversal.

conclusion: Within Gide (n=30) the de-novo antigen-presentation axis separates ICB response at LOO AUROC ~0.87-0.89, beating an infiltration score (0.80) — the n=12 pilot's AUROC=1.0 was small-sample optimism, and the honest within-cohort estimate is ~0.88.
The response signal is LARGELY INFILTRATION-DRIVEN: after fold-contained residualization on an immune-infiltration score, the tumor-intrinsic residual is only LOO ~0.62-0.64.

CRITICAL NEGATIVE: the axis does NOT replicate across cohorts. Trained on Gide it scores AUROC 0.36-0.44 on held-out Riaz. Within Gide it separates cleanly (R median +0.70 vs N -0.59, AUROC 0.91); within Riaz there is NO separation (R median +0.32 vs N +0.11, AUROC 0.40 — indistinguishable from chance at n=5v5). This is non-replication, not a sign reversal, and is not a batch/scaling artifact. Only the infiltration score shows weak cross-cohort consistency (~0.68).

Interpretation for the thesis: a single-phenotype, gene-level de-novo axis is cohort-specific and mostly reflects microenvironment, NOT a transferable tumor-intrinsic RNA state. Testing the actual thesis requires the multi-phenotype WES-blind features (IR, editing, TE/ERV) from the pipeline session; gene-level antigen-presentation expression alone is insufficient and does not generalize.
